_meta.name: CLM r5 execution plan tracker — post-AOT-unblock
_meta.author: Aiden

blockers_resolved.summary: hexa 인터프리터 OOM on 3504-LOC train_clm.hexa — Mac local `hexa build` AOT success: 2.1s wall, 172MB RSS peak, /tmp/clm.bin 404KB Mach-O arm64. Smoke exit 0: 3 train_step finite loss + CE + phi gate + CLM-PhiB-2 PASS.

smoke_results.known_quirk: println("34m approximate params:", _d34_total) emits label but omits int value — stage1 codegen multi-arg println quirk with complex int expr. No PASS/FAIL impact.

next_actions.desc: Re-run `hexa build training/train_clm.hexa -o /tmp/clm-linux.bin` on hetzner for Linux x86_64 binary

result.binary_format: ELF 64-bit LSB pie x86_64 dynamically linked
result.clang_warning_type: expression-result-unused (same stage1 codegen quirk as Mac, harmless)

smoke_result.note: @link Mac .dylib path rewritten to libhxblas.so / libhxlmhead.so by runtime.c hexa_ffi_dlopen Linux branch (basename + <dir>/lib<name>.so)

next_actions.desc: mmap-equivalent byte-window corpus loader — exec("dd") per window path audited + inlined into train_clm.hexa L36-81

result.inlined_subset_in_train_clm: training/train_clm.hexa L36-81 (corpus_open + corpus_window only, hot path)
result.inline_rationale: pod2 relaunch scaffold transpiles a fixed DEP list; avoid module-resolution surgery (feedback_hexa_silent_exit_5_imports)
result.ffi_native_mmap_deferred: real mmap(2) FFI would need hexa C-shim; current dd+od path is page-cache friendly (~32 KiB/step resident); swap-in path documented in clm_mmap_loader.hexa header
result.smoke_evidence: [clm_mmap_loader] opened /root/Dev/anima/data/corpus.txt len=5243172 bytes (~5 MiB) — appears twice per run (load_corpus + _current_corpus_desc)

next_actions.desc: Wire --config JSON + --resume into train_clm.hexa CLI dispatch. Instead of a separate clm_r5_launch.hexa wrapper (would triple the dep bundle), flags land on the existing binary so one bundle = one fire.

result.binary: /tmp/clm_v3_linux.bin (hetzner ELF x86_64, rebuilt from train_clm.hexa with r5-a3 wiring)
result.clang_warning_type: expression-result-unused (stage1 codegen quirk, harmless, unchanged from v2)

config_parser.scalar_extractor: _cfg_json_read_scalar / _cfg_json_read_int / _cfg_json_read_float — mirrors clm_val.hexa:json_read_scalar, handles quoted strings + bare numbers
fields_mapped: steps / save_every (picked up only when CLI value matches default literal)
config_parser.override_semantics: JSON fields override --scale template defaults; explicit CLI --batch/--lr/--steps/--save-every override JSON (detected via string-equals-default sentinel)
config_parser.smoke_log_line: [train_clm] config applied: name=clmsmoke d=256 nl=4 nh=8 kv=4 blk=128 batch=4 lr=0.0003 steps=1 save_every=1

resume_support.scan: _resume_find_last_step(ckpt_dir) — shell: ls step_* | sed extract trailing integer | sort -n | tail -1, returns -1 for missing
resume_support.semantics: counter-only resume (scans + logs last step; training continues with fresh weights). Full weight restore deferred to r5-a3.5 (load_checkpoint() HEXACKPT-v1 decoder not yet wired). Sufficient to prove launcher plumbing end-to-end for smoke.
resume_support.smoke_log_line: [train_clm] --resume: last ckpt step=0 in /tmp/clm_v3_smoke (counter-only resume; weights fresh)

config_smoke.note: 1 step on small-config (d=256 L=4 blk=128, same geometry as scale_smoke) proves --config scalar extraction end-to-end. 170M config (d=768 L=12 blk=512) parsed correctly but per-step CPU wall exceeded 10 min; GPU fire required for 170M+ smokes.

known_quirks_inherited_from_r5_a1: Multi-arg println with int value: '[train_scale] step=' emits label but omits step number (stage1 codegen). Ckpt meta.json still carries step correctly.
--save-every 1 triggers MFS rotation on each save; fine for smoke, pod fire uses save_every >= 2000 per runpod_mfs_quota rule.

next_actions.desc: training/clm_r5_bundler.hexa — stage + tarball + R2 upload OR direct-scp modes (r5 §1.3 option A)

result.corpus_handling: R2 → pod via rclone on pod (NOT packed into tarball — 5.38GB would bloat bundle)
result.auto_pull_hetzner: Auto-scp from hetzner:/tmp/clm_v2_linux.bin + /home/hexa-lang/self/native/build/*.so when not present locally

next_actions.desc: Pod fire H100 with Linux binary + corpus + bundled deps (prerequisite: a1-a4)
next_actions.owner: user confirm (r5 design: not yet PREP → FIRE)

prerequisites_for_fire: a1 Linux binary built on hetzner
forbidden_shortcuts: Do NOT run `hexa run training/train_clm.hexa` on any host (interpreter OOM known, kernel hang risk)
Do NOT skip Linux rebuild and scp Mac arm64 binary to H100 x86_64 pod
Do NOT modify train_clm.hexa to reduce LOC purely to fit interpreter (AOT is the fix, not source truncation)
